rationale: In the theoretical background, the cited paper is used in the key argument that closing a mental account is more psychologically important than opening one, alongside related foundational references. That premise is central to the citing paper's mechanism: trade-in consumers prioritize the used-product transaction and become more tolerant of higher new-product prices. Although it appears in a grouped citation, its role is conceptual support for core hypothesis development rather than a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Soman and Gourville (2001) as part of its mental-accounting foundation for why account-closing outcomes carry extra weight. It uses that logic to motivate its theory that trade-ins shift attention toward the used product value and increase willingness to pay for the new product.
